title: lepidagathis psilantha
intro: Lepidagathis psilantha, also known as the 'Savandera plant', is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of Lepidagathis psilantha are used in Ayurvedic medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from the plant's essential oils and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Lepidagathis psilantha are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
